title: Multi Line Item Quote

posts.author: rbanchero (Robert Banchero)
posts.cookedHtml:
We’re trying to create quotes within Monday that include several (sometimes 100+) line items. An example quote may have 5 products, with each item have dozens of specific components. Subset of an example file attached.

We’d like to upload this excel data directly to Monday CRM and generate a customer facing quote. The Monday.com UI seems limited to adding a single line item at a time which isn’t realistic given how many each of our quotes have.

Anyone doing anything similar or have recommendations on how to add several line items at once?

Screenshot 2025-05-03 at 10.51.07 PM1904×714 156 KB

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: Check out an app called Docugen. That’s what I use for our customer facing quotes.

posts.author: DocCreate (FlowTech Apps)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello Robert, Check out the DocCreate app available on the monday.com marketplace. The app allows you to easily create quotes using the info in your board. There are no limits to the # of line items that can be added to a single quote. You can reach out to DocCreate to help you setup your board and app integration through a free consultation: support@doccreate.io.

posts.author: johannesgeyer (Johannes Geyer)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @rbanchero

I think there’s no easy answer for your request. Usually we would recommend using the line item for the quote/client information and subitems for the products like in this example:

  • Generate Quotes & Invoice with monday.com


Unfortunately, subitems are limited to 100 and therefore, you could create a board per quote.

Our app DocExport PDF Generator also supports loading data from other boards which could be useful, if each of your products always have the same components. Otherwise it would be very manual.

If hope this gives you already a few ideas on how you could structure your boards.

Best regards,
Johannes

posts.author: Flowtech (Flowtech Apps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @rbanchero

You’re right — monday.com’s UI isn’t ideal for creating quotes with 100+ line items manually.

Here’s a faster approach:

  1. Import Line Items in Bulk
  • Upload your Excel file to a dedicated Line Items board
  • Use Connect Boards + Mirror Columns to link them to your main Quotes board
  • Or automate via Monday API, Make, or Zapier
  1. Generate a Custom Quote PDF with DocCreate
  • Pulls all linked line items into a professional PDF
  • Supports logo, branding, layout control, headers/footers, and page breaks
  • Auto-generates on item creation or status change

Demo: Watch here
Install DocCreate: Click to Install

Let me know if you want help setting up a working example!
